text: In the first scene, I feel like an observer on the threshold—watching others in quiet discussion through glass, slightly removed yet present. This separation gives rise to a meditative, almost liminal mood. In the second scene, surrounded by words and ideas about research and contemplation, I feel invited into intellectual curiosity. The environment urges reflection, as if the space itself is encouraging me to ponder connections between knowledge, uncertainty, and creativity. Here, philosophical inquiry feels not only possible, but welcomed. The subtle interplay between observation and engagement leaves me poised within possibility, attentive and alive to thought.
